preamble: Backing of the voiced velar fricative [ɣ] to the voiced uvular fricative [ʁ] is a common process in languages with a back-oriented consonant system.

examples.note: In some Dutch dialects, the voiced velar fricative is backed to a uvular position.
Historical developments in Arabic and its dialects show shifts between velar and uvular voiced fricatives.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
